display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: A retention number is going on a slide

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is the audience asking
'is the book growing?'
or 'are we keeping customers?'

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
Is expansion concentrated
in a few large accounts?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
LEAD WITH GRR
the honest retention floor - caps at 100%
exposes the leak NRR hides

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SHOW NRR AND GRR PLUS CONCENTRATION
NRR alone masks the tail churn
name the expansion concentration risk

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
NRR IS DEFENSIBLE AS HEADLINE
but still SHOW GRR beside it
broad expansion is real growth

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is a logo / dollar churn
problem suspected or reported?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
GRR FIRST, ALWAYS
NRR will hide the churn behind expansion
GRR isolates the leak
